replication_study.hypothesis: Among Asian subjects in Experiment 1, making ethnicity salient (givenprimingquestionnaire==1) reduces the log of the continuously compounded reservation interest rate (latent discount rate). Operationally: in an interval regression of the latent log reservation interest rate (bounds lndiscratelo, lndiscratehi) on givenprimingquestionnaire and controls, the coefficient on givenprimingquestionnaire is negative.
replication_study.study_type: Experimental (laboratory experiment; analyzed as observational experimental data)

data_plan.dataset_identifier: REPExperiment1Data.dta (processed Experiment 1 data provided in replication_data folder)
data_plan.source_type: Processed experimental dataset (Stata .dta) derived from in-person lab experiments
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Experiment 1 data; replication will subset to Asian subjects (asian == 1). (Both-rounds variants exist in folder: REPExperiment1DataBothRounds.dta and REPExperiment1DataR2.dta.)
data_plan.sample_size: Primary replication focuses on the Asian subject sample in Experiment 1. The authors' .do claims list 71 Asian subjects (see ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do and ReadMe). The dataset contains repeated choice observations per subject (choice-level observations: 1688 rows in REPExperiment1Data.dta). Unit-level subjects are clustered in estimation.
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Choice-level intertemporal observations (interval-censored) clustered by individual subject (id); primary inference clusters by subject.
data_plan.access_details: Data file provided in the repository at data/original/31/input/replication_data/REPExperiment1Data.dta. No external access request required for these provided files.

qualification.explanation: This dataset contains the original processed Experiment 1 data and the exact variables used to estimate the focal coefficient (treatment indicator and interval bounds on log reservation interest rate). It is therefore qualified to reproduce the original analysis and verify the focal claim. Evidence: variable names and descriptions for lndiscratelo/lndiscratehi, givenprimingquestionnaire, and asian appear in the replication_data ReadMe (ReadMe.pdf) and are used in the provided Stata .do scripts (ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do, RepTable2BothRounds.do). The processed .dta file (REPExperiment1Data.dta) is included in replication_data and contains these variables (see loaded variables list and data inspection).
qualification.similarity_to_original: High-fidelity: the dataset is the authors' processed Experiment 1 data (variable definitions and processed variables match the ReadMe.pdf descriptions and the .do scripts). Specific references: ReadMe.pdf (variable definitions for lndiscratelo, lndiscratehi, treatE/givenprimingquestionnaire, asian) and ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do (the .do uses lndiscratelo/lndiscratehi and givenprimingquestionnaire for intreg on asian == 1). Files inspected: data/original/31/input/replication_data/ReadMe.pdf and data/original/31/input/replication_data/ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do.
qualification.deviation_from_original: This replication uses the same processed data released with the paper rather than an independent new sample. Therefore it is a reproduction (re-running the original analysis on the authors' released data) rather than an independent empirical replication. The original paper describes experiments run at Harvard/UMich/Temple; the provided processed .dta is the derived data used to produce tables (see ReadMe.pdf and the .do files). The provided files indicate the sample composition claims (e.g., 'Participants were Harvard College undergraduates, 71 of Asian descent...' in ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do), indicating this is the original processed dataset rather than a different dataset. (References: replication_data/ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do and replication_data/ReadMe.pdf.)

data_plan.notes: Notes and caveats: (1) Data are interval-censored: outcomes are provided as lower and upper bounds (lndiscratelo, lndiscratehi). (2) Observations are repeated per subject and therefore standard errors must be clustered by id. (3) The provided Stata .do files assume Stata environment and paths on the original author's machine; code paths have been adapted to /app/data for Python execution. (4) There are also BothRounds files (REPExperiment1DataBothRounds.dta) if the user wants alternate pooling; current plan uses REPExperiment1Data.dta (single experiment/round processed file) consistent with the .do that generates the focal results.

steps: 2) Subset to Asian subjects (asian == 1) as done in ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do.
3) Keep interval outcome bounds lndiscratelo (lower) and lndiscratehi (upper). Handle right-censoring where upper bound is missing as the .do implies.

4) Estimate an interval regression (equivalent to Stata intreg) on the interval-censored latent log interest rate using regressors: givenprimingquestionnaire (treatment indicator), largestakes, longterm, largelong, and intercept. Cluster standard errors by subject (id).

5) Compute cluster-robust standard errors. Because direct closed-form cluster-robust standard errors for custom MLE interval-regression code are non-trivial in Python, implement cluster bootstrap (resampling subjects with replacement) to obtain clustered standard errors consistent with the original clustering by id.

6) Report coefficient on givenprimingquestionnaire (estimate, clustered SE, t-statistic, p-value), and compare magnitude/direction to the original reported coefficient (e.g., ~ -1.4165 reported in initial details).

7) Optionally replicate alternative specifications and subgroup checks exactly as in the provided .do scripts (e.g., separate by stakes/term combinations, exclude skeptical subjects if variables available).
planned_method.models: Interval regression (interval-censored regression of latent normal variable) using maximum likelihood (equivalent to Stata intreg). Implementation in Python via custom MLE maximizing log-likelihood of normal latent variable over intervals.
planned_method.outcome_variable: Latent log continuously compounded reservation interest rate (interval bounds: lndiscratelo, lndiscratehi).
planned_method.independent_variables: givenprimingquestionnaire (ethnicity-salience treatment indicator) as primary regressor; controls: largestakes, longterm, largelong; intercept.
planned_method.control_variables: largestakes (indicator for larger stakes), longterm (indicator for long-term tradeoffs), largelong (interaction of large stakes and long term) — as used in the .do scripts and ReadMe.pdf.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10 (or 3.9+). Key packages: pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels (optional), pyreadstat (for robust .dta reading if needed). I created the Python analysis script data/original/31/input/replication_data/replicate_experiment1__py.py that implements interval-censored MLE and cluster bootstrap to approximate Stata intreg clustered estimates.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Estimate coefficient (beta) on givenprimingquestionnaire (treatment effect) in the interval regression; report cluster-robust standard error (via cluster bootstrap), sigma (latent error sd) and log-likelihood.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Two-sided t-test of coefficient using cluster-bootstrapped SE; report t-statistic and p-value. Use also 95% confidence interval from cluster bootstrap SE.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Exclude observations missing the lower bound (these are not usable). For observations missing upper bound (right censored), handle appropriately in likelihood (treat as P(Y >= lower) = 1 - Phi((lower - mu) / sigma)). If covariate values are missing, drop those observations (listwise deletion) unless a small number and imputation warranted; document dropped observations count.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Primary replication focuses on one pre-registered focal test (coefficient on givenprimingquestionnaire among Asians). No multiple-testing correction for primary test. If conducting numerous subgroup/specification checks, adjust reporting (Bonferroni or Benjamini-Hochberg) and clearly label exploratory analyses.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Primary significance threshold alpha = 0.05 (two-sided). Report robustness to alpha = 0.01 as noted in the original paper. Also compare direction and magnitude to original reported effect (direction must match; effect magnitude reported as large negative in original).

files.replicate_experiment1__py.py: Python script placed in data/original/31/input/replication_data/. It (a) loads /app/data/original/31/input/replication_data/REPExperiment1Data.dta, (b) subsets to asian == 1, (c) constructs regressors (intercept, givenprimingquestionnaire, largestakes, longterm, largelong), (d) fits interval-censored MLE analogous to Stata intreg (normal latent errors) and (e) computes cluster-robust standard errors via cluster bootstrap resampling of subject ids. It writes results JSON to /app/data/replication_results_experiment1.json. This is the executable entrypoint for the replication analysis.
files.ReplicationExperiment1_Claims.do: Original Stata .do included for reference; shows the intended Stata intreg commands and sample restrictions (e.g., 'if asian == 1' and use of lndiscratelo/lndiscratehi and givenprimingquestionnaire). NOT executed directly; used to verify exact model specification.
files.RepTable2BothRounds.do / RepTable2.do: Reference Stata .do files for generating Table 2 specifications and subgroup regressions. Useful for additional checks (these .do files use intreg with cluster(id) for certain specs).

codebase.notes: I created replicate_experiment1__py.py (written to data/original/31/input/replication_data/) to provide a Python execution path, as required by the run policy. The original .do files reference local paths on the original author's machine; the Python script uses /app/data paths and the provided .dta file. Dependencies include pandas and scipy. The script stores outputs under /app/data to comply with IO rules. If users prefer to run Stata directly, the provided .do files are available but their file paths must be adapted to the environment (/app/data).

analysis.instructions: 1) Ensure data files are available at /app/data/original/31/input/replication_data/REPExperiment1Data.dta. 2) From inside container or environment with dependencies installed, run: python /app/data/original/31/input/replication_data/replicate_experiment1__py.py . 3) The script loads data, subsets asian==1, fits interval regression MLE, runs cluster bootstrap to obtain clustered SEs, and writes results JSON to /app/data/replication_results_experiment1.json. 4) Report coefficient for givenprimingquestionnaire and cluster-bootstrap SE, t-statistic, p-value, and number of clusters (subjects) and observations. 5) Optionally run additional specs mirroring the .do files (separate stakes/term regressions and both-round variants using REPExperiment1DataBothRounds.dta).
analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare focal coefficient on givenprimingquestionnaire (estimate), clustered standard error, t-statistic and p-value to the original reported number (initial_details.txt reports coefficient ≈ -1.4165 with clustered SE ≈ 0.3783; ReadMe/.do show intreg on lndiscratelo/lndiscratehi with givenprimingquestionnaire). Also compare implied median reservation rates: authors reported a drop from ~8.8% to ~2.1% in some comparisons (post_registration.json results). Report whether sign, magnitude (order), and statistical significance match.
